heading: Conveyances carrying liquor
label: 3669 Conveyances carrying liquor
text: Any conveyance, whether used by the owner or another in introducing or attempting to introduce intoxicants into the Indian country, or into other places where the introduction is prohibited by treaty or enactment of Congress, shall be subject to seizure, libel, and forfeiture.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Indians (, ).section 247 of title 25Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 146, § 139 Stat. 970Words “Automobiles or any other vehicles or” at beginning of section were omitted, and “any conveyance” substituted to remove possible ambiguity as to scope of section.Words at conclusion of section “provided in ” added nothing and were therefore omitted. (See also rule 41 of the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure.)section 246 of this titleMinor changes were made in arrangement and phraseology.
